page: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical AMD EPYC Milan core operating at 2.0 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The instance is provisioned with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local SSD storage. It operates under the QEMU hypervisor and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%, and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores and small-block read-only memory bandwidth registering in the bottom 10%. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also reflect this limitation, scoring in the poor and weak categories respectively.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation, the ccx13 offers predictable compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads requiring stable CPU performance without the virtualization noise of shared-core instances. However, the low memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling present clear tradeoffs for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU execution but do not demand high memory throughput.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs from a single physical AMD EPYC Milan core running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, 80 GB of local SSD storage, and runs on the QEMU hypervisor. While the server delivers strong single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks, its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with memory read-only bandwidth and database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. This hardware profile offers a qualitatively cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without shared-core interference. The ccx13 is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC Milan processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering stable single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated virtual server with 2 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD storage, optimized for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated virtual server offering stable single-core AMD EPYC performance and local SSD storage for lightweight, predictable workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC Milan CPU with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Lower memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling limits
Well-suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads and basic web serving
